highlight: First season under Reynolds and McElhenney. The takeover completed mid-season as the new owners began laying the foundations for the club's rise.
Narrowly missed automatic promotion behind Stockport County (94 pts). Lost a dramatic playoff semi-final to Grimsby Town 5-4 after extra time. Paul Mullin scored 26 league goals.

Champions! Wrexham stormed to the title with a record 111 points — the most ever in England's top five divisions. Paul Mullin scored 38 league goals. Near-perfect home record (22W, 1D, 0L). Ended a 15-year exile from the EFL.

Back-to-back promotions! Clinched with a 6-0 demolition of Forest Green Rovers. Finished behind Stockport County (92 pts, champions). Paul Mullin scored 24 league goals. Arthur Okonkwo made 40 appearances on loan from Arsenal.

Historic third consecutive promotion — the first team ever to achieve this in England's top five tiers. Beat Charlton Athletic 3-0 to clinch it. January signings Sam Smith and Jay Rodriguez proved crucial. Championship bound!

Wrexham's first Championship season since 1982. Sitting in the playoff zone after a record summer spend of around £40 million. Marquee signings include Nathan Broadhead, Callum Doyle, and Kieffer Moore. Into the FA Cup Fifth Round after beating Nottingham Forest on penalties and Ipswich Town 1-0.
